paper_metadata.title: GLM: General Language Model Pretraining with Autoregressive Blank Infilling
paper_metadata.abstract: There have been various types of pretraining architectures including autoencoding models (e.g., BERT), autoregressive models (e.g., GPT), and encoder-decoder models (e.g., T5). However, none of the pretraining frameworks performs the best for all tasks of three main categories including natural language understanding (NLU), unconditional generation, and conditional generation. We propose a General Language Model (GLM) based on autoregressive blank infilling to address this challenge. GLM improves blank filling pretraining by adding 2D positional encodings and allowing an arbitrary order to predict spans, which results in performance gains over BERT and T5 on NLU tasks. Meanwhile, GLM can be pretrained for different types of tasks by varying the number and lengths of blanks. On a wide range of tasks across NLU, conditional and unconditional generation, GLM outperforms BERT, T5, and GPT given the same model sizes and data, and achieves the best performance from a single pretrained model with 1.25x parameters of BERT Large , demonstrating its generalizability to different downstream tasks.

extraction_data.organization: Tsinghua University, Beijing Academy Of Artificial Intelligence (Baai), Mit Csail, Shanghai Qi Zhi Institute
extraction_data.innovation: GLM introduces autoregressive blank infilling with 2D positional encodings and span shuffling, unifying NLU, conditional, and unconditional generation tasks under a single pretraining framework. It outperforms BERT, T5, and GPT on a wide range of tasks with the same model size and data.
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, conditional text generation, unconditional text generation, cloze question answering
extraction_data.research_problem: Unified pretraining framework for natural language understanding (NLU), unconditional generation, and conditional generation tasks
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Cloze question answering for NLU tasks
extraction_data.extension: 2D positional encodings and span shuffling for autoregressive blank infilling, enabling arbitrary order prediction of spans.
extraction_data.paper_title: GLM: General Language Model Pretraining with Autoregressive Blank Infilling

extraction_data.organization: Tsinghua University, Beijing Academy Of Artificial Intelligence (Baai), Mit Csail, Shanghai Qi Zhi Institute
extraction_data.innovation: GLM introduces autoregressive blank infilling with 2D positional encodings and span shuffling, unifying NLU, conditional, and unconditional generation tasks under a single pretraining framework. It outperforms BERT, T5, and GPT on a wide range of tasks with the same model size and data.
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, conditional text generation, unconditional text generation, cloze question answering
extraction_data.research_problem: Unified pretraining framework for natural language understanding (NLU), unconditional generation, and conditional generation tasks
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Cloze question answering for NLU tasks
extraction_data.extension: 2D positional encodings and span shuffling for autoregressive blank infilling, enabling arbitrary order prediction of spans.
extraction_data.paper_title: GLM: General Language Model Pretraining with Autoregressive Blank Infilling

extraction_data.parameters: 110M, 110M (APPROXIMATE, INFERRED FROM BERT BASE COMPARISON), 117M, 340M, 340M (APPROXIMATE, INFERRED FROM BERT LARGE COMPARISON), 410M, 515M, 10B
extraction_data.innovation: GLM introduces autoregressive blank infilling as a pretraining objective, unifying NLU and generation tasks by reformulating classification tasks as blank infilling. This method avoids the independence assumption of masked language modeling (MLM) and enables better modeling of interdependencies between masked tokens.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: BookCorpus, Wikipedia, CC-News-en, OpenWebText2, Stories (RoBERTa dataset, total 158GB)
extraction_data.extension: Autoregressive blank infilling allows the model to predict spans of tokens in a sequence, enabling both bidirectional context and generative capabilities.
extraction_data.research_problem: General language model pretraining for diverse tasks including natural language understanding, text generation, and blank infilling
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, sequence-to-sequence tasks, text infilling, language modeling, abstractive summarization, question generation
extraction_data.paper_title: GLM: General Language Model Pretraining with Autoregressive Blank Infilling

extraction_data.innovation: GLM introduces autoregressive blank infilling, a pretraining objective that masks and predicts spans of text in an autoregressive manner, enabling bidirectional context understanding and generative capabilities within a single model.
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, sequence-to-sequence tasks, text infilling, language modeling, abstractive summarization, question generation
extraction_data.research_problem: General language model pretraining for diverse tasks including natural language understanding, text generation, and blank infilling
extraction_data.extension: 2D positional encoding for long text generation
extraction_data.paper_title: GLM: General Language Model Pretraining with Autoregressive Blank Infilling
